questions.text: What is the core structural difference between an autonomous agent loop and standard prompt-response generation?
options: The agent loop dynamically executes actions based on feedback from the environment and continues processing iteratively.
The agent loop does not use embeddings.
The agent loop is immune to hallucination.
questions.correct_answer: The agent loop dynamically executes actions based on feedback from the environment and continues processing iteratively.
questions.explanation: Agent loops run in iterations, processing observations from tool executions to decide subsequent actions, rather than completing in a single turn.
